payload.title: Replay protection accepts the already-trusted generation
payload.body: `validateVerifierBindings` rejects a root only when its generation is less than `trustedGenerationHighWater`. A root whose generation equals the high-water mark is therefore accepted again. An attacker able to replay a previously valid signed root can reintroduce that generation after it has been invalidated or superseded, potentially restoring stale authority, security, or serving policy. Require the candidate generation to be strictly greater than the trusted high-water mark (`<=` must fail), or separately track idempotent verification versus activation so an equal generation can never be republished.
